title: Harden Predict trading edge cases and recovery states
description: Add robust handling for lifecycle, pricing, balance, and indexing edge cases across open and close flows. The goal is to prevent users from signing doomed transactions and make post-submit state understandable when the public server lags the chain.
acceptance_criteria: As a trader, I see disabled actions and specific reasons when an oracle is inactive, expired before settlement, settled, or missing price/SVI data. As a trader, I see clear errors for insufficient manager balance, insufficient wallet DUSDC, insufficient position quantity, invalid strike, invalid quantity, quote asset mismatch, paused trading, and ask bound rejection. As a trader, after submit I can open the transaction in Sui explorer and the UI refreshes direct chain state before relying on indexed server state. As a trader, repeated clicks, wallet rejection, network failure, and server lag do not leave duplicate or misleading pending actions.

title: Implement close and redeem position flow
description: Build the user-facing redeem workflow for existing directional positions. Support live redeem using predict::redeem and settled redeem behavior after checking oracle lifecycle and manager position quantity.
acceptance_criteria: As a trader with an open position, I can choose a full or partial quantity to close. As a trader, I see redeem payout, remaining quantity, and whether the payout is live-priced or settled before signing. As a trader, I can redeem successfully when I own enough manager quantity and the oracle is quoteable or settled. As a trader, I get clear blocking states for insufficient quantity, expired-but-not-settled oracle, zero payout, stale preview, failed transaction, and delayed server refresh.

title: Implement wallet portfolio positions for close flow
description: Add a wallet-focused positions or portfolio view backed by manager summary, manager positions summary, and current oracle state. The view should show only the connected trader's open position quantities and give the close flow the data it needs for redeem previews.
acceptance_criteria: As a connected trader, I can see my open BTC/DUSDC positions grouped by oracle, expiry, strike, and UP/DOWN side. As a connected trader, I can see quantity, estimated redeem payout, expiry, settlement status, and last refresh time. As a trader, I can filter out global historical activity and focus on my manager-owned positions. As a trader, empty and server-lag states explain whether I have no manager, no positions, or pending index refresh.

title: Implement open position flow on oracle market page
description: Turn the existing oracle trade preview into a complete BTC/DUSDC position mint workflow. Reuse the strike, side, quantity, and pricing preview controls, then add wallet connection, manager readiness, balance sufficiency, submit, confirmation, and refresh states.
acceptance_criteria: As a trader viewing an active BTC oracle, I can choose UP or DOWN, strike, and quantity from valid market grid values. As a trader, I see estimated cost, manager balance impact, and stale-preview warnings before signing. As a trader with enough manager DUSDC, I can mint the selected position and see the transaction result. As a trader, I cannot submit when the oracle is not tradeable, quantity is invalid, quote balance is insufficient, ask bounds reject the trade, or trading is paused.

title: Build manager funding flow for DUSDC trading balance
description: Add the UX and transaction path for moving DUSDC from the connected wallet into the user's PredictManager via predict_manager::deposit, including balances, validation, and post-submit refresh. This is required because predict::mint debits manager balance, not wallet coin balance directly.
acceptance_criteria: As a trader with DUSDC in my wallet, I can deposit a chosen amount into my PredictManager. As a trader without enough DUSDC, I see the deficit before signing. As a trader, I can distinguish wallet DUSDC balance from manager DUSDC balance. As a trader, after a successful deposit the balance refreshes and opening a position can continue.

title: Add generated Predict bindings and transaction helpers
description: Replace ad hoc raw Move targets for new trading flows with generated or centralized Predict bindings for create_manager, predict_manager::deposit, predict::mint, predict::redeem, and predict::redeem_permissionless. Keep current supply/withdraw helpers working while making transaction construction testable.
acceptance_criteria: As an app developer, I can build manager creation, manager deposit, mint, and redeem PTBs from one typed helper layer. As a trader, mint transactions pass Predict, PredictManager, OracleSVI, MarketKey, quantity, and Clock correctly. As a trader, redeem transactions choose normal or permissionless settled redeem according to oracle state and ownership rules. As a maintainer, helper tests cover MarketKey construction, quantity scaling, and call targets for BTC/DUSDC testnet.

title: Add PredictManager account discovery and lifecycle
description: Build the wallet-side trading account layer for DeepBook Predict. The app needs to find a user's existing PredictManager from the indexed server, create one on-chain with predict::create_manager when absent, and expose manager id, owner, quote balance, and position-bearing state to trade screens.
acceptance_criteria: As a connected trader, I can see whether I already have a PredictManager. As a connected trader without a manager, I can create one and continue without manually pasting object IDs. As a connected trader, I can see my manager quote balance before opening or closing positions. As a trader, I get a clear empty, loading, error, and server-lag state after manager creation.

title: Decide first-release Predict trading scope
description: Resolve product and protocol assumptions before implementation of DeepBook Predict position open/close flows. Current uncertainty includes whether v1 supports directional positions only or ranges too; whether the app should auto-create a PredictManager; whether it should include manager deposit/withdraw UX or only trade from an existing manager balance; how users obtain DUSDC; whether redeem should support live, settled, and permissionless settled flows; slippage/preview freshness policy; and what portfolio filters are required for BTC/DUSDC.
acceptance_criteria: As a trader, I know whether v1 supports only BTC/DUSDC directional UP/DOWN positions or also ranges. As a trader, I know whether the app creates and funds my PredictManager automatically or expects an existing manager. As a trader, I know how DUSDC acquisition and manager deposits are handled before mint. As a trader, I know whether closing supports partial redeem, full redeem, settled redeem, and permissionless settled redeem. As a trader, I know what preview freshness, stale data warning, and transaction failure copy should do.
notes: Human interview questions: 1) Should v1 support only directional UP/DOWN positions for BTC/DUSDC, or also vertical ranges? 2) Should the app auto-create a PredictManager when absent? 3) Should DUSDC deposits into PredictManager happen inline in the open-position flow, or as a separate wallet/account page? 4) Should close support partial quantities in v1? 5) Should settled positions use owner-only redeem, permissionless redeem, or both? 6) What slippage or stale-preview tolerance is acceptable between preview and signed transaction? 7) Should v1 include DUSDC acquisition/faucet guidance, or assume the wallet is already funded?

title: make list of beads tasks for open/close DeepBook Predict positions
description: users want to use risk-react-web app to open (mint) and close (redeem) deep book predict positions for BTC/DUSDC pair. Split this feature implementation into tasks, interview human of all gaps in requiremnets or implementation in as many iterations as required to cover all cases including edge cases. Each task must have user stories as acceptance criteria
notes: Created child task breakdown risk-trading-3ec.1 through risk-trading-3ec.9 for DeepBook Predict BTC/DUSDC open/close flows. External docs checked: Predict package README at github.com/MystenLabs/deepbookv3/tree/predict-testnet-4-16/packages/predict. Key implementation assumptions captured in child tasks: positions live inside PredictManager, mint debits manager quote balance, redeem pays back into manager, public server is render backend, direct chain reads are needed around wallet writes. bd dolt pull failed because no Dolt remote is configured in this workspace.
close_reason: Created child Beads task breakdown for DeepBook Predict BTC/DUSDC open/close flows and filed human-needed decision bead risk-trading-3ec.1 for remaining requirement gaps.

title: Fix LP flow local-day aggregation
description: LP flow groups by UTC date but labels by local date, which can render duplicate local days with split totals. Update chart bucketing so the grouping key and displayed label use the same calendar day.
close_reason: Fixed LP flow grouping to use local-day keys matching displayed labels

title: Correct PLP decimal display scale
description: Update risk-react-web so PLP parsing and formatting uses 6 decimals instead of 9, matching the corrected token scale.
close_reason: Corrected PLP token decimals to 6 and verified build

title: on /vaults/activity page supply and withdrawal amounts are x1000 times smaler that they should, remember DUSDC has 10^6 decimal points not 10^9
close_reason: Fixed DUSDC activity amount formatting to use 6 quote decimals

title: Fix /vaults displays the same page as /vautls/activity. When done check if you need to update risk-trading-8mw beads task/issue
close_reason: Fixed by making /vaults/activity a non-nested route; verified /vaults renders Vault Actions and /vaults/activity renders Liquidity Pool Activity.

title: Verify Predict trading with tests and funded testnet wallet
description: Add automated coverage where feasible and a focused manual validation pass for the full BTC/DUSDC open-close workflow against testnet. Include a funded wallet path because wallet signing and DUSDC availability cannot be fully proven with unit tests alone.
acceptance_criteria: As a maintainer, transaction helper tests verify manager, deposit, mint, and redeem PTB construction. As a maintainer, frontend tests cover validation and disabled states for open and close flows. As a tester with a funded testnet wallet, I can create or reuse a manager, deposit DUSDC, mint a small BTC/DUSDC position, redeem it, and confirm indexed history updates. As a maintainer, known testnet prerequisites and any faucet/manual setup steps are captured in the issue notes or app docs.

title: move "vault activity" table from /vaults/activity page to /vaults page right under "LP Flow" card; remove /vault/activity page
close_reason: Moved vault activity into /vaults and removed dedicated activity route

title: Fix LP flow chart outlier scaling
description: LP flow bars become unreadable when one supply or withdrawal bucket is orders of magnitude larger than the rest. Add a scale mode that preserves raw tooltip values while making normal buckets visible.
close_reason: Implemented symmetric log scaling, linear toggle, raw tooltips, and outlier markers for LP flow chart.

title: Audit DUSDC display formatting in risk web
description: Check DUSDC/USD display call sites in apps/risk-react-web, confirm base-unit scaling for position amounts, and centralize token formatting helpers where appropriate.
close_reason: Completed DUSDC/base-unit formatting audit and fixes in risk-react-web

title: check if 'LP Flow' chart converts DUSDC to base points, DUSDC uses 10^6 decimal points so 1000000000 of DUSDC should be formated as
close_reason: Fixed LP Flow chart to convert DUSDC base units with 6 quote decimals

title: add inflow/outflow bar chart for lp (vault) supplies and withdrawals on @apps/risk-react-web/src/routes/vaults.tsx
close_reason: Added LP inflow/outflow chart to vaults page

title: Show oracle quote formula
description: Add a compact formula note under the oracle quote preview explaining estimated cost and redeem payout calculations from get_trade_amounts.
close_reason: Added compact formula note under the DUSDC amount note in the oracle quote preview.

title: Add quantity stepper to oracle quote preview
description: Add minus and plus controls beside the oracle quote preview quantity input so users can adjust quantity without typing.
close_reason: Added plus and minus icon buttons to adjust quote preview quantity by 1 with a zero floor.

title: Filter oracle quote strikes by direction
description: Filter the oracle quote preview strike buttons so UP shows strikes above spot and DOWN shows strikes below spot, while keeping a nearest-strike fallback if the sampled rows do not contain an actionable side.
close_reason: Filtered quote preview strikes by UP/DOWN side relative to spot with nearest-strike fallback.

title: Add oracle strike quote preview
description: Add a strike selection UI to oracle detail pages that previews estimated DUSDC mint/redeem amounts from predict::get_trade_amounts for a user-entered quantity.
close_reason: Implemented oracle strike quote preview with quantity, direction switch, and DUSDC mint/redeem preview.

title: Build read-only SVI oracle dashboard
description: Add a first version of a DeepBook Predict oracle page by URL param that fetches oracle state, computes local SVI fair UP/DN probabilities, and displays decision charts/tables for read-only position evaluation.
close_reason: Implemented first read-only SVI oracle dashboard

title: Add DeepBook Predict vault liquidity actions
description: Add user-facing supply and withdraw flows for DeepBook Predict vault liquidity. Current app is read-only and only shows oracle, position, and LP activity from predict-server testnet endpoints. Missing pieces: Sui wallet connection, Sui client/network config, package/object/coin type constants, onchain Predict and vault reads, quote coin and PLP balance discovery, transaction builders for predict::supply and predict::withdraw, form validation, previews for vault value/shares/withdrawable amount, transaction signing/execution, post-transaction refresh, error states, and tests/build verification. Docs: https://docs.sui.io/onchain-finance/deepbook-predict/contract-information/predict
acceptance_criteria: Users can connect a Sui wallet on the configured network; users can see accepted quote asset balance, PLP balance, vault value, and currently withdrawable amount; users can submit a supply transaction that deposits quote asset and receives PLP; users can submit a withdraw transaction that burns PLP and receives quote asset; invalid or unavailable amounts are blocked before signing; successful transactions link to explorer and refresh balances/activity; build passes.
close_reason: Implemented DeepBook Predict vault liquidity supply/withdraw UI, Sui wallet/client providers, onchain vault and wallet balance reads, transaction builders, validation, explorer links, route navigation, dApp Kit CSS, and build verification. Live funded-wallet transaction testing split to risk-trading-0m9.

title: Trim trailing zeros in amount formatting
description: Update money and decimal amount display so insignificant trailing decimal zeroes are omitted while preserving meaningful fractional digits.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add liquidity pool activity route
description: Create a /vaults/activity page that fetches LP supplies and withdrawals, merges and sorts them by checkpoint, and displays the activity table in navigation.
close_reason: Implemented /vaults/activity route with merged LP supply and withdrawal table

title: Add sidebar navigation to risk React web
description: Add shadcn sidebar navigation for the existing app routes and verify the app still builds.
close_reason: Added sidebar navigation with working active state and verified build.

title: Add positions route
description: Add /positions page with opened and closed tabs backed by predict-server minted and redeemed position endpoints.
close_reason: Implemented /positions route with opened and closed position tables.

title: Switch shadcn components to Radix UI
description: Migrate shadcn setup and existing UI primitives from Base UI to Radix UI.
close_reason: Implemented Radix UI shadcn migration for tabs.

title: Pin risk-react-web latest package versions
description: Replace latest dependency specifiers in apps/risk-react-web/package.json with the versions already resolved in pnpm-lock.yaml, and keep lockfile specifiers aligned.
close_reason: Pinned latest dependency specifiers to installed lockfile versions.

title: Add Oracle States table state tabs
description: Add active/settled tab filtering to the Oracle States table on the risk React web index route, preserving existing query params and defaulting invalid state params to active.
close_reason: Implemented Oracle States active/settled tab filter

title: Add oracles state table
description: Add a shadcn table to apps/risk-react-web/src/routes/index.tsx that displays oracle state data from the Mysten predict-server oracles endpoint.
close_reason: Implemented oracle states table and verified production build

title: Manually test Predict vault liquidity with funded testnet wallet
description: Run an end-to-end browser test of the /vaults page using a funded Sui testnet wallet that owns DUSDC. Verify wallet connect, supply signing/execution, PLP balance refresh, withdraw signing/execution, quote balance refresh, and explorer transaction links. This follows the implemented DeepBook Predict vault liquidity UI in risk-trading-yvk.
acceptance_criteria: A funded testnet wallet can supply DUSDC to the Predict vault and receive PLP; the same wallet can withdraw by burning PLP and receive DUSDC; balances and explorer links update after each transaction.
close_reason: Completed
